title: Why do I choose isolation? — Sep 27 2025
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-week-into-Austin journal entry: Yahir interrogates the pattern of voluntary-isolation directly. "I am in the middle of the US, I know nobody, I left my loving family, friends I made in Oregon. For what?? To feel immensely lonely and feel like you are moping around your day."
  • Five hypothesis-questions posed to himself: do I do it to feel something? Internal gauntlet/challenge? Masochism? To appreciate my people via absence? — and ends without a chosen answer, which is the point.
  • Names the temperament: "my nature calls for me to wander outwards and away. To be plopped in an unknown and figure it out." This is the explicit articulation of voluntary adversity as philosophy in Yahir's own first-person voice.
  • Suicide proximity flagged in plain language: "This shit is a dangerous thing to play with, it really is. This is how people commit suicide. Suicide." Comes ~11 months after David's suicide and is a tonal echo Yahir is consciously aware of.
  • Resolution micro: "there is no time for the wicked, you are here right now. Breathe in the moment and go." — the same be-here-now break-glass move he uses across thoughts-and-things entries.

Beats

  • First time named in source: "This is my first time." — meaning first time being properly alone, no pre-existing social network. The Austin move as the actual first solo-adversity gauntlet, not just rhetorical.
  • 17 lines total, dense — sits between the Sep 20 on Austin arrival reflection and the Oct 4 stranger doc as the valley of the arrival arc.

Privacy

  • Pure self-interrogation. No third parties. Suicide-adjacent language present in source — preserved here because the Fallen cluster context makes it load-bearing.

Relations

  • 2025 09 20 on Austin — week-1 arrival reflection
  • 2024 10 13 Davids suicide — the suicide-language echo target
  • 2025 11 24 Get a Grip — "I am a total loser outsider" extends this isolation

  • founder loneliness 2022 2026
